title: Can Evangelions reproduce
wikitext:
[]: Can Evangelions reproduce?             Quote Post by skikes » Wed May 05, 2010 7:46 pm Adam is the "Seed of life" to all Angels. Lilith is the "Seed of Life" to all of humanity. Evangelions are clones of the Seeds. Can Evangelions produce offspring just like those they are cloned from? A form of life derived from Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed May 05, 2010 7:54 pm They're females with prostates. You tell me.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed May 05, 2010 7:54 pm I wouldn’t think so. They weren’t created for breeding, just for fighting the angles.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed May 05, 2010 7:55 pm Thanks a lot, child of Lilith. I immediately wanted to delete my post but you responded like a second after I posted and now I can't.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed May 05, 2010 7:57 pm If you didn’t like your post to begin with then you shouldn’t have posted it in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed May 05, 2010 7:59 pm Evangelions not being impotent would make me rather uncomfortable if I was a NERV techie and it happened to be mating season.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Wed May 05, 2010 8:14 pm Instant no. There's armor in the way! :P

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed May 05, 2010 8:19 pm Ovaries where? I figured that the cloning process just did away with all those unnecessary reproductive organs.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed May 05, 2010 9:49 pm I think the idea of evas and/or angels getting jiggy is horrifyingly awesome. Now, where the hell are Reichu and Defectron when we need them?

[]:             Quote Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Wed May 05, 2010 10:16 pm I think it would make sense if 01 could reproduce the way Lilith did, but I'm not certain about the others. It would be asexual reproduction though, not...that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 05, 2010 10:29 pm Can Evangelions reproduce?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed May 05, 2010 10:57 pm Inb4 Reichu posts pics of Evas having sex (surely you have some...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 06, 2010 12:31 am @skikes : We can surmise that every Eva has the potential to create life, for the reason you state, but exactly what needs to be done to make that happen is a total unknown. -- Now to address the other posts and entertain fannish whimsies. Merridian wrote: I figured that the cloning process just did away with all those unnecessary reproductive organs.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Fri May 07, 2010 8:59 am If Seele are worried about Eva's becoming too powerful (as in with the s2 engine issue) they really wouldn't give them the ability to reproduce. I always thought it was odd that all Eva's are female as the groin of the army looks clearly male.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 07, 2010 9:27 am I think if the Evangelions could reproduce, it would be like when the Alien Queen gave birth to the newborn in Alien Resurrection.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 07, 2010 9:35 am TheLobe wrote: If Seele are worried about Eva's becoming too powerful (as in with the s2 engine issue) they really wouldn't give them the ability to reproduce.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 07, 2010 10:33 am Reichu wrote: You're cruel, Aaron......

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri May 07, 2010 11:36 am If Evangelions reproduce, IT'LL BE THE END OF ALL OF US! NOOOOO!

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Fri May 07, 2010 11:47 am Yes, didn't you see that deleted scene where Eva unit 01 faps to unit 02 and then growls sadly?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri May 07, 2010 4:28 pm Reichu wrote: It's just a piece of armor covering the pelvis that happens to have a projecting doohickey on it. The actual look of it varies from artist to artist as well. Sometimes it's no more than a blade, i.e., bares absolutely no resemblance to a "codpiece". It implies about as much about what it covers as (e.g.) the Evas' giant jaw pieces imply about the actual size of their jaws.
